description: Baseline: simple OTA + pseudo-R HPF + 2nd-order Sallen-Key. .ends broken by evaluate.py
Two-stage Miller opamp + 4th-order Sallen-Key, but inputs swapped → positive feedback
Fixed opamp polarity. f_high=45Hz (nF caps destroying opamp phase margin)
R=10MΩ + pF caps. Pseudo-R DC convergence failure → hpf_out railed
Ideal 100GΩ R replaces pseudo-R. Filter works! Ripple=4dB, power=13.8µW
100GΩ R, reduced M7, wider diff pair. Only noise fails (114µV vs 100)
6th-order Butterworth at fc=170Hz. Ripple=0.81dB! Noise=102µV (barely fails)
Wider diff pair W=12u, slightly higher bias. Noise=86µV, all specs pass!
Reduced M7 W=4u→3u: power 9.09→7.30 µW. All 6/6 pass, better margins.
Wider diff pair W=20u: noise 86→72 µVrms. Power 7.3 µW. All pass.
Fine-tune C2=90pF. f_high=167.5, ripple=0.78, noise=72, power=7.3
DISCARDED: 8th-order Butterworth. Q=2.56 too high for opamp GBW → ripple=1.49 dB
Wider diff pair W=30u: noise 72→64 µVrms. All margins comfortable.
INVESTIGATED: Chebyshev Type I — Q=6.51 too high for opamp. Butterworth is optimal.
Section 3 C1=370pF: stopband 21.7→22.2 dB. Better balanced margins.
C1=380pF, best balanced margins (min 11%)
Diff pair L=2u: noise 66→60 µVrms. Ripple now tightest at 9%.
v2: 8th-order SK Butterworth at fc=180. ripple=0.65, atten=26.0, noise=65, power=6.7
Fix ECG peak detection (was detecting T-waves). R-peak error 42%→0.9%
Robustness test: bias/HPF caps robust, S4 sensitive (expected for Q=2.1)
Reduce bias 4.0M→4.5M: power 6.72→6.00 µW (40% margin). All PVT pass.
